claim_text: NVTS reported 2026Q1 revenue of $8.6M, GAAP gross margin -9.3%, non-GAAP gross margin 39.0%, GAAP operating loss $27.8M, non-GAAP operating loss $11.7M, and cash/equivalents $221.0M.
NVTS guided 2026Q2 revenue to $10.0M +/- $0.5M, non-GAAP gross margin to 39.25% +/- 75 bps, and non-GAAP opex to $14.5M-$15.5M.
Management said high-power markets represented a large majority of revenue and grew approximately 35% YoY.
notes: Segment detail is limited; treat as management disclosure.

claim_text: Operating cash flow was negative $16.362M for 2026Q1, versus negative $13.533M in 2025Q1.
Distributor A represented 59% of 2026Q1 revenue and 58% of accounts receivable at March 31, 2026.
The YoY revenue decline was primarily due to lower mobile sales in Asia, primarily China, and consumer markets.
Management said high-power markets drove the return to sequential growth and are expected to continue driving sequential growth through 2026.
notes: Transcript source is third-party but captures call remarks.

claim_text: Management framed GaN and SiC content per MW as meaningful in AI data-center 800V architectures.
Power Integrations reported 2026Q1 revenue of $108.3M, +3% YoY and +5% QoQ, operating cash flow of $20.0M, industrial revenue +23% YoY, and Q2 revenue guidance of $115M-$120M.
Public market data showed NVTS around $29 and market cap around $6.8B in late May 2026.
notes: Market data can move intraday; refresh before trading.

claim_text: NVIDIA said it is leading a transition to 800 VDC data-center power infrastructure to support 1MW IT racks and beyond starting in 2027, and listed Navitas among multiple silicon providers.
notes: Important: partner list is broad; not exclusive to NVTS.

claim_text: Navitas announced collaboration with NVIDIA on next-generation 800 V HVDC architecture to support Kyber rack-scale systems such as Rubin Ultra, using GaNFast and GeneSiC technologies.
notes: Strong architecture validation, but not the same as named production revenue.

claim_text: Navitas said it is developing 100V GaN FETs, 650V GaN, and high-voltage SiC devices for NVIDIA's 800 VDC AI factory architecture and that 100V GaN FETs use 200mm GaN-on-Si through Powerchip.
Infineon announced collaboration with NVIDIA on 800V HVDC power systems and highlighted grid-to-core expertise across Si, SiC, and GaN.
notes: Shows NVTS is not the only strategic silicon supplier.

claim_text: Texas Instruments unveiled a complete 800 VDC power architecture with NVIDIA, including 800V-to-6V and 6V-to-<1V stages.
ST described NVIDIA-related 800 VDC delivery boards, including 6kW and 20kW boards using wide-bandgap devices, and said NVIDIA validated ST's 12kW proof-of-concept board for production testing.
ROHM said it is among silicon providers supporting NVIDIA's 800V HVDC architecture and described SiC/GaN products aligned to high-voltage power conversion.
